deep_dive.title: Ingredient-Level Food Image Segmentation for Nutrition Awareness
deep_dive.abstract: Food images often contain several visible ingredients, so assigning one dish label to an entire image hides important visual structure. This work studies ingredient-level semantic segmentation on FoodSeg103, where the model predicts an ingredient class for each pixel. Two SegFormer variants were fine-tuned and evaluated under a controlled setup: SegFormer-B0 as the smaller baseline model and SegFormer-B1 as the larger final model. Both models use ImageNet-pretrained MiT backbones with newly initialized 104-class output layers. On the held-out FoodSeg103 test split of 2,135 images, B0 achieved 0.7709 pixel accuracy and 0.2521 mean IoU, while B1 achieved 0.7929 pixel accuracy and 0.3204 mean IoU. B1 improved every saved test metric, including a +0.0683 absolute gain in mean IoU. The system also converts predicted masks into visible ingredient-area percentages, giving a simple visual composition summary of the predicted meal. This summary can serve as a first-pass nutrition-awareness cue by providing a visual alternative to detailed food tracking similar to plate-based meal guidance, but it is not a direct estimate of calories, macronutrients, food mass, volume, density, or true portion size.

deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向营养意识的食品图像成分级分割
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向营养意识的食品图像成分级分割
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper studies ingredient-level semantic segmentation on FoodSeg103 using SegFormer variants, achieving 0.7929 pixel accuracy and 0.3204 mean IoU, directly supporting food image analysis for nutritional composition prediction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作研究食品图像中可见成分的像素级语义分割，以改善营养信息获取。通过在FoodSeg103数据集上微调SegFormer-B1模型，实现了0.7929像素准确率和0.3204平均交并比，相比基线模型有显著提升。此方法为基于图像的食物营养成分预测提供了关键的分割基础，有助于深度学习膳食评估系统的发展。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该工作研究食品图像中可见成分的像素级语义分割，以改善营养信息获取。通过在FoodSeg103数据集上微调SegFormer-B1模型，实现了0.7929像素准确率和0.3204平均交并比，相比基线模型有显著提升。此方法为基于图像的食物营养成分预测提供了关键的分割基础，有助于深度学习膳食评估系统的发展。
